subject: Mathematics
chapter: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Solid Shapes)

key_points: Euler’s Formula ($F + V - E = 2$) links faces, vertices, and edges of any polyhedron
Slicing a solid creates a 2D 'cross-section' (horizontal or vertical)
Shadow play involves projecting 3D shapes onto 2D surfaces as shadows
Viewing solids from different angles: Top (Plan), Front (Elevation), and Side views
examples: Euler's Rule: For a square pyramid, $5 (F) + 5 (V) - 8 (E) = 2$
Cross-section: Slicing a cone vertically through the tip results in a triangle
Shadow: A cylinder can cast a circular shadow (from top) or rectangular (from side) [Image showing shadows of a cylinder from different light angles]
Views: The top view of a house shows only the roof layout
formulas: For Prisms $\rightarrow V = 2n, F = n+2, E = 3n$ (where $n$ is sides of the base)
exam_tips: Always verify your F, V, and E counts using Euler's formula to catch mistakes
Think of a cross-section as a 'bread slice' through the object
For shadow problems, identify which 2D shape matches the widest part of the solid
Practice 'Visualizing' the hidden back edges of a solid when looking at 2D drawings
